title: Oat and honey face mask
subtitle: A soothing single-use dry mask, mixed fresh each time
excerpt: A dry face mask mix of finely milled oat, kaolin clay, and honey powder stored in a jar. Each use combines one tablespoon of the dry mix with a small amount of water or yoghurt to make a paste. No preservative is needed because the water is added fresh for each use.
sourceNotes: Rosemary Gladstar, Herbal Recipes for Vibrant Health (2008), Ch. 5, dry facial powder bases. Bramble Berry kaolin clay notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026).

content.text: An oat and honey dry face mask. The dry mix is made in bulk and kept in a jar; each use mixes one tablespoon with a small amount of water to make a paste. The dry-mix approach solves the shelf-life problem of water-containing face masks: the dry ingredients keep 12 months, and the water-containing paste is made fresh immediately before each use.

The finely milled oat gives a gentle scrub and soothes dry or sensitive skin. The kaolin clay soaks up a little oil without the drying effect of stronger clays like bentonite. The honey powder is dried honey, so the mix stays dry in the jar. Once you stir it with water, the honey draws moisture into the skin like fresh honey does.

items.name: Colloidal oatmeal
items.prepNote: finely milled oat or colloidal oatmeal. 50% of the dry mix. If using rolled oats, grind in a spice grinder to a fine flour before measuring.

items.name: Kaolin clay
items.prepNote: white kaolin clay. 33% of the dry mix. The mildest cosmetic clay; suitable for dry and sensitive skin.

items.name: Honey
items.prepNote: honey powder (dehydrated honey). 17% of the dry mix. Available from cosmetic suppliers. Fresh honey can be substituted at the mixing stage instead (add a small amount with the water).

content.text: Weigh all three dry ingredients into a bowl and stir to combine evenly. Kaolin clay is fine and can clump; break any lumps with the back of a spoon and stir until the blend is homogenous. Transfer to a clean, dry jar with a tight-fitting lid.
To use: mix a single-use paste

Scoop 1 tablespoon (approximately 5-6 g) of the dry mix into a small cup or the palm of a clean hand. Add 1-2 teaspoons of water, warm water is more comfortable on the face, and mix to a smooth paste. The paste should be spreadable but not runny. Apply to clean, damp skin, avoiding the eye area. Leave for 10-15 minutes. Rinse thoroughly with warm water and pat dry.

Dab a small amount of the mixed paste on the inside of the wrist and wait 24 hours before using on the face. Oat is in the grass family. People with bad grass pollen allergies may react. Honey holds trace pollen. People with bee-sting allergies should know the honey is in the mix, though honey on the skin at this level is usually safe even for mild bee-sting sensitivity.

Dry mix: 12 months in a sealed jar in a cool, dry cupboard. Never introduce a wet spoon or wet hands into the dry-mix jar, which would cause clumping and mould. Mixed paste: use immediately and discard the remainder. Any remaining paste is water-containing and will grow bacteria within hours.

recipe.yieldDescription: 60 g dry mask mix (~8-12 single-use masks)
